mission: Map Instance Bootstrap Path in opencode
key_architectural_finding: The instance bootstrap is a TWO-TIER system. The core package (packages/core/) provides Effect-TS services (schemas, models, event bus, plugin registry). The actual DB, config resolution, MCP connections, tool registry, and session management live in the EXTERNAL SDK (@opencode-ai/sdk/v2/client) which communicates with the app via HTTP. The app (packages/app/) orchestrates bootstrap by fetching data from the SDK and populating local Solid.js stores. There is NO database code, NO config file reader, NO MCP client, and NO tool registry in the workspace packages.

phase_map.notes: Creates XDG directories (data, config, state, tmp, log, bin, repos) synchronously on module load. Sets up Flock global and computes path constants. Blocking — must succeed before any file I/O.

Creates ManagedRuntime with memoized layer maps. Observability layer (OTLP) conditionally enabled via OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT flag. Logger layer always present. This is the Effect runtime used by all core services.

Effect Layer DAG that initializes core services in dependency order: EventV2 → PluginV2 → Catalog → AgentV2 → AccountV2 → AISDK → Npm. PluginBoot forks-scoped plugin registration (EnvPlugin → AccountPlugin → ProviderPlugins → ModelsDevPlugin) and exposes wait() to await completion. Uses Effect.withSpan('PluginBoot.boot') for tracing.

Registers 28+ AI provider plugins (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Bedrock, etc.) into the PluginV2 registry. Each provider plugin defines a 'catalog.transform' hook that injects models into the Catalog during registration. Can degrade — individual providers can fail without blocking others.

App-side phase. Resolves server connection (HTTP, Sidecar, SSH). Performs startup health check with grace period for non-HTTP connections (Effect.timeoutOrElse with 10s duration). Blocking for HTTP connections, background for others. If health check fails, shows error page. Runs BEFORE any SDK calls.

Fetches global (non-project-specific) data from SDK: global config, provider list, path info, project list. Uses @tanstack/solid-query for caching. Uses retry() wrapper from core. Writes results to query cache and global Solid.js store. Errors are collected but not blocking — showErrors() displays toasts.

Per-project instance bootstrap. Runs independently for each opened directory. Two tiers of loading: FAST (sessions, agents, config, session status, project ID, path) and SLOW (providers, MCP, LSP, VCS, session diffs, permissions, questions, todos, commands, project meta). Missing MCP/LSP data degrades gracefully (mcp_ready/lsp_ready flags). Shows error toast for slow-load failures but instance remains usable.

Loads session list from SDK, estimates root session total, applies session trimming (SESSION_RECENT_LIMIT=50, 4-hour window). Session prefetch warms recent sessions. Individual session loads are independent and can fail without blocking others.

Sets up per-directory child stores for real-time sync. Creates Solid.js reactive stores for sessions, messages, parts, todos, permissions, questions, diffs. Subscribes to SDK events via event-reducer. Implements eviction (MAX_DIR_STORES=30, DIR_IDLE_TTL_MS=20min) and dispose checks.

MCP and LSP are SDK-managed services. The app queries status via SDK calls (sdk.mcp.status(), sdk.lsp.status()) and sets mcp_ready/lsp_ready flags. MCP server configs (local commands, remote URLs) are defined in the SDK, not in the workspace. The app has type definitions in packages/app/src/types/mcp.ts for McpServerConfig (local/remote). Can degrade — instance operates without MCP/LSP.

Subscribes to SDK event streams for real-time updates. Event reducer applies incoming events (session updates, message additions, permission requests, etc.) to local Solid.js stores. Uses createRefreshQueue for batching. Runs continuously after bootstrap completes.

entrypoint_files.description: Global filesystem paths and directory creation. Module-level initialization of XDG directories. Exports Global Service (Effect Context.Service) with home, data, cache, config, state, tmp, bin, log, repos paths.

Plugin boot sequence. Registers EnvPlugin → AccountPlugin → ProviderPlugins → ModelsDevPlugin. Provides Service with wait() to await completion. Uses Effect.forkScoped + Deferred for async registration. Traced with Effect.withSpan('PluginBoot.boot').

Plugin registry (PluginV2). Hooks-based architecture with 7 hook types: catalog.transform, account.switched, aisdk.language, aisdk.sdk, agent.update, agent.remove, agent.default. Uses immer for draft-based hook output mutation.

Provider+Model catalog. Combines provider plugins' catalog.transform hooks into a unified registry. Provides model selection (smart pick preferring small models by default), provider/model lookup, and event emission for provider/model changes.

Event bus (EventV2). PubSub-based typed event system. Supports publish, subscribe (per-type channels), all stream, sync handlers. Events have typed schemas via define(). Registry for event definitions.

Provider schema definitions. Defines ProviderV2.Info with well-known provider IDs (opencode, anthropic, openai, google, etc.), endpoint types (openai/responses, openai/completions, anthropic/messages, aisdk, unknown), and options (headers, body, aisdk config).
Model schema definitions. Defines ModelV2.Info with capabilities, cost, variants, limits. Includes parse() for providerID/modelID string splitting.
Agent definitions (AgentV2). Agent registry with get/list/update/remove/defaultInfo/defaultAgent operations. Integrates with PluginV2 for agent.update, agent.remove, agent.default hooks.
Account credential management (AccountV2). OAuth and API key credentials stored in JSON files. Supports add/list/remove/activate operations. Emits AccountV2 event stream.
AI SDK service (AISDK). Bridges ModelV2.Info to @ai-sdk/provider LanguageModelV3. Resolves language models via plugin hooks (aisdk.language, aisdk.sdk). Handles OpenAI-compatible SSE wrapping.

OTLP observability. Conditionally enables OpenTelemetry tracing and logging when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set. Uses @effect/opentelemetry for traces, OtlpLogger for logs. Registers AsyncLocalStorageContextManager for non-Effect code span context propagation.
Effect logger layer. JSON-structured logging with fields (service, span, etc.). Uses core util/log for persistence.
Effect ManagedRuntime factory. Creates runtime with memoized layer maps. makeRuntime() combines service layer + observability layers.

Provider plugin index. Aggregates 28+ provider plugins (Alibaba, AmazonBedrock, Anthropic, Azure, Cerebras, Cloudflare, Cohere, DeepInfra, Dynamic, Gateway, GitHubCopilot, GitLab, Google, GoogleVertex, Groq, Kilo, LLMGateway, Mistral, Nvidia, OpenAI, OpenCode, OpenRouter, Perplexity, SAP, TogetherAI, Venice, Vercel, XAI). Each plugin registers catalog.transform hooks.
ModelsDev plugin. Registers development/local models into catalog. Uses ModelsDev data source to discover ollama, lmstudio, and other local model servers.
Location-scoped service map. LayerMap that provides Catalog and PluginBoot services keyed by Location.Ref. Used to create scoped instances with their own catalog+boot lifecycle.
Project ID resolution. Resolves project ID from Git remote URL (preferred), cached opencode file, or Git root directory. Falls back to 'global' project ID. Supports VCS (git) metadata.

App entry point. Solid.js app with provider hierarchy: QueryClient → ServerConnection → Settings → Language → Servers → Platform → ServerSDK → ServerSync → Ambassador → Permission → Models → Layout → Highlights → Inspector → Notification → SessionProviders. ConnectionGate performs server health check before rendering.

Bootstrap orchestrator. bootstrapGlobal() loads cross-project data. bootstrapDirectory() loads per-project data with fast/slow tier separation. Uses @tanstack/solid-query for fetching, retry() for resilience, and Solid.js batch() for reactive store updates.
Server sync context. Creates per-directory sync contexts with SDK client, session loading, MCP/LSP query functions, and event subscriptions. Manages BootProgress tracking.
Server connection management. Resolves server list (HTTP, Sidecar, SSH connections). Provides server deduplication, URL normalization, and connection health checking.

Global sync types. Defines State (the per-directory reactive store shape), VcsCache, MetaCache, IconCache, DirState, EvictPlan, DisposeCheck, RootLoadArgs. Defines constants: MAX_DIR_STORES=30, DIR_IDLE_TTL_MS=20min, SESSION_RECENT_WINDOW=4h, SESSION_RECENT_LIMIT=50.
Child store manager. Creates and manages per-directory reactive stores with eviction policies. Tracks directory access times, loads sessions, subscribes to SDK events, and manages store disposal.

Directory-level sync. Manages optimistic updates for messages and parts. Uses createStore with produce/reconcile for immutable updates. Implements part deduplication (SKIP_PARTS), message sorting, and inflight request management.
MCP type definitions (app-side). Defines McpLocalConfig (command, environment, timeout, enabled) and McpServerEntry (name, config). McpRemoteConfig imported from SDK.
Service use helper. Creates cached Effect service accessors for Context.Service tags. Used for efficient repeated service access within Effect programs.
LayerMap example/pattern. Demonstrates the pattern for scoped config services using Effect LayerMap. Shows ConfigServiceMap with per-context config resolution and invalidation.

Feature flags via Effect Config. Reads environment variables and config for feature toggles: OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS, OPENCODE_CLIENT, OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR, etc.

files.description: OpenTelemetry tracing layer. Provides OTLP trace export via @effect/opentelemetry/NodeSdk. Exports enabled (boolean), resource() (service metadata), layer (Effect Layer for tracing+logging). Only active when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT flag is set.
files.pattern: Named exports + namespace re-export: 'export const Observability = { enabled, layer }'

files.description: Effect Logger layer. JSON-structured logging with service/span/timestamp fields. Uses core util/log for persistence. Supports custom fields via Handle interface.
files.pattern: Named exports. logger is a Logger.make(), layer wraps it.

files.description: ManagedRuntime factory. Provides makeRuntime() that combines service layers with observability/logger layers.

naming_conventions.export_pattern: Top-of-file re-export: 'export * as ModuleName from "./file"' (used in almost every core source file)

error_handling.pattern: Schema.TaggedErrorClass for domain errors (e.g., NotFoundError, InvalidDefaultError, FileWriteError, InstallFailedError)
error_handling.propagation: Effect.fail(new ErrorType({...})) — tagged errors with structured data
error_handling.degradation: Effect.catchTag("ErrorType", handler) or Effect.ignore for non-critical failures
error_handling.span_errors: Effect.exit + Deferred.done for capturing both success and failure in async forks (used in PluginBoot)

persistence_format.format: JSONL (util/log.ts for logs), OTLP JSON (for observability traces/logs when enabled)
persistence_format.jsonl_pattern: util/log.ts provides structured JSON logging to filesystem
persistence_format.trace_backend: OTLP HTTP exporter (batch span processor) when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set

key_types_and_conventions: Effect Context.Service pattern: every service is a class extending Context.Service<Service, Interface>("@opencode/...")
Layer composition: defaultLayer chains Layer.provide() to satisfy dependencies
Schema.Struct / Schema.Class for data models (Effect Schema)
withStatics() utility for adding static methods to branded schemas
immer's produce() for immutable state updates in Plugin hooks and Agent updates
HashMap for in-memory registries (agents, plugins, models)
PubSub for event streaming (event bus, plugin added notifications)
Scope / forkScoped for managed async lifecycle

packages/core/src/.description: Core Effect-TS services, schemas, and models. No DB, no HTTP server, no file config reading.
top_level_files: global.ts - Global paths and directory init
session.ts - Session ID schema (ONLY — no session mgmt)

packages/app/src/.description: Solid.js app UI. Orchestrates bootstrap via SDK HTTP calls. Manages reactive stores.

key_dirs.context/global-sync/: Bootstrap, child stores, session load, event reducer, queue, eviction, session cache, session prefetch, session trim
key_dirs.context/file/: File tree, content cache, watcher, path management

gaps.description: The actual DB, config file resolution, MCP server management, tool registry, and session persistence all live in @opencode-ai/sdk/v2/client which is an EXTERNAL npm package. The phase map above covers only what's visible in this workspace. At least 5-7 phases are missing from the map because they run inside the SDK: config file reading/merging, DB init/migrations, MCP server spawning/connection, tool registration/assembly, session CRUD. To complete the phase map, the SDK source must be inspected.
gaps.impact: HIGH — without SDK inspection, the phase map is incomplete. The app-side phases (bootstrapGlobal, bootstrapDirectory) are fetchers, not the actual boot logic.

gaps.description: The sidecar process lifecycle is opaque. The app connects to a sidecar (Sidecar server type) or HTTP server. How the sidecar starts, initializes its own Effect runtime, loads DB, and begins listening is not in the workspace.
gaps.affected_files: Unknown — likely in SDK or a separate binary
gaps.impact: MEDIUM — the sidecar is the backend that owns the instance. Without understanding sidecar startup, the full bootstrap chain is incomplete.

gaps.description: The app fetches config via sdk.config.get() but the actual config resolution (opencode.json/toml reading, schema validation, defaults application, env var overrides, merging) is all in the SDK. No config reading code exists in the workspace.
gaps.affected_files: None in workspace — all in external SDK
gaps.impact: MEDIUM — config resolution is a key bootstrap phase that's entirely opaque.

gaps.description: No drizzle, better-sqlite3, or migration code exists in the workspace. drizzle-orm appears only as an override in core/package.json. The database initialization and migration phase is entirely in the SDK.
gaps.impact: HIGH — DB init is a critical bootstrap phase. No database code exists in the traced workspace.

gaps.description: Tools (bash, read, write, glob, grep, etc.) are not defined in the workspace. The only tool-related code in core is tool-output.ts (Tool.TextContent, Tool.FileContent — just output schemas). GitHub Copilot tool definitions exist but those are GitHub-specific, not opencode built-in tools. The actual tool registration, assembly, and execution lives in the SDK.
gaps.impact: HIGH — tool registry is a central bootstrap concern. Not in workspace.

gaps.description: MCP server configuration types are defined in app/src/types/mcp.ts but the actual MCP client (connection, spawning, tool listing, error handling) is entirely in the SDK. The app only queries status via sdk.mcp.status().
gaps.affected_files: packages/app/src/types/mcp.ts (types only), packages/app/src/context/server-sync.tsx (loadMcpQuery — status fetch only)
gaps.impact: MEDIUM — MCP is mentioned but the actual connection logic is external.

gaps.description: There is no 'Instance' class or object in the workspace. The concept of an instance is implicit — it's the combination of a connected SDK client + per-directory reactive store + bootstrap state. The workspace uses 'directory' as the instance identifier, not 'instance'.
gaps.affected_files: All bootstrap code uses 'directory' as the key

gaps.description: There is NO dedicated startup trace module in the workspace. Tracing exists only as Effect spans (withSpan) and OTLP observability. No StartupTrace, SidecarTrace, BootPhase, or structured startup phase tracking exists. Any startup trace implementation would need to be created from scratch.
gaps.affected_files: None — no startup trace module exists
gaps.impact: CRITICAL — if the mission is to add startup tracing, there is no existing pattern to follow. The implementation must create new conventions.

conventions_summary.export_pattern: Every source file starts with 'export * as ModuleName from "./file"' (self-re-export pattern). All exports are named. No default exports.
conventions_summary.service_pattern: Effect Context.Service<Service, Interface>("@opencode/v2/Name") with Layer.effect() construction and defaultLayer re-export.
conventions_summary.schema_pattern: Effect Schema for all data models. Schema.Class for complex types, Schema.Struct for inline types. Branded types for IDs.
conventions_summary.error_pattern: Schema.TaggedErrorClass for domain errors. Effect.fail() for propagation. Effect.catchTag() for targeted handling.
conventions_summary.async_pattern: Effect.gen(function* () {...}) for async effects. Deferred for completion signals. Scope/forkScoped for lifecycle management.
conventions_summary.tracing_pattern: Effect.withSpan() for named spans within Effect programs. OTLP for export when enabled. No structured startup phase tracing.
conventions_summary.naming_convention: kebab-case filenames, PascalCase service names, camelCase functions. Self-re-export at file top.
